empirical.interpretation: Pre-registered (ADR-0042) routing ON (ELC) vs OFF (vanilla GPT-BERT-class) bake-off, 10M Strict-Small, official scorer, 2 seeds/arm, identical otherwise. Official BLiMP: ELC 61.32/61.64 (mean 61.48, CV 0.37%) vs vanilla 62.74/62.55 (mean 62.65, CV 0.21%). Delta(vanilla-elc)=+1.17 >= +1.0 -> DECISION: adopt VANILLA (--no-layer-routing) as the v0.2 backbone; ELC every-layer routing costs ~1.2 BLiMP on the leaderboard scorer and is retired. Pāṇinian mechanisms graft onto vanilla. Both arms seed-stable at 10M (CV < 0.5%; folded M0c satisfied). Both v0.2 arms beat v0.1 official SS (59.46); vanilla 62.65 approaches the SS baseline 65.08 (the ~2.4pt gap is the leading-indicator target for M2 rigour + M3 ACD circuits). 100M backbone-confirm folded into M4.

integrity.tarka_memo: Strongest objection: n=2 seeds per arm is thin, so the +1.17 gap could be seed noise. Resolution: the gap (+1.17) exceeds each arm's seed spread (ELC 0.32, vanilla 0.19) and is consistent in direction across both seeds (vanilla > elc for both); CV < 0.5% both arms. A second objection: vanilla loses slightly on BLiMP-supplement (57.79 vs 58.36) — but BLiMP is the pre-registered primary metric and the leaderboard headline; supplement is secondary and within noise. Third: 10M may not predict 100M (F1 shows scale-dependence) — mitigated by folding the 100M backbone-confirm into M4 (first finals run validates vanilla at scale; if it regresses, that is an M4 intervention, not a silent assumption).

_note: M1 architecture DECISION milestone. technical/empirical/integrity/memory satisfied. artifacts.paper_section + demos + sign-off land at the empirical milestones (paper bump = M4). The backbone is locked (vanilla); M2 (Pāṇinian rigour) and Lane-C (ACD) build on it; the 100M backbone-confirm is folded into M4.
